text: PostgreSQL uses MVCC to allow concurrent readers without blocking writers.
Indexes accelerate row lookup at the cost of extra writes during insert and update.
A B-tree index is the default in most relational engines for ordered range scans.
Transactions provide ACID guarantees: atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability.
DuckDB is a single-file columnar OLAP engine embedded in the host process.
Foreign-key constraints enforce referential integrity between related tables.
Sharding partitions data across multiple nodes to scale horizontally.
Replication keeps secondary nodes in sync with the primary for read scaling and failover.
The query planner picks an execution plan based on table statistics and estimated cost.
Vacuum reclaims dead tuples left behind by updates and deletes in MVCC engines.
Gradient descent updates model parameters in the direction that reduces the loss.
Cross-entropy loss measures the divergence between predicted and true class distributions.
Regularization techniques like L2 weight decay reduce overfitting on the training set.
Dropout randomly zeroes activations during training to encourage redundant representations.
Batch normalization rescales intermediate activations to stabilize training of deep networks.
Convolutional neural networks share weights across spatial positions for image recognition.
Recurrent networks process sequences one step at a time while maintaining a hidden state.
Transformers replace recurrence with self-attention so every token can look at every other token.
Embedding models map text into dense vectors so semantic similarity becomes cosine similarity.
Fine-tuning adapts a pretrained model to a downstream task with a smaller labeled dataset.
TCP provides reliable, ordered, byte-stream delivery between two endpoints.
UDP is connectionless and lossy, used when low latency matters more than reliability.
The three-way handshake establishes a TCP connection: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK.
TLS encrypts the TCP payload and authenticates the server via X.509 certificates.
DNS resolves human-readable hostnames into IP addresses through a hierarchy of nameservers.
HTTP/2 multiplexes many request-response pairs over a single TCP connection.
QUIC runs over UDP and combines transport, congestion control, and TLS in one protocol.
A reverse proxy terminates client connections and forwards traffic to backend servers.
Load balancers distribute incoming requests across a pool of healthy backend instances.
NAT lets many private IP addresses share a single public IP by rewriting source ports.
Public-key cryptography uses a key pair where one key encrypts and the other decrypts.
A hash function produces a fixed-size digest that is hard to invert and collision-resistant.
HMAC combines a hash function with a secret key to authenticate message integrity.
Salted password hashing prevents rainbow-table attacks by making each hash unique.
JWT tokens carry signed claims so a server can validate them without a session database.
SQL injection happens when user input is concatenated into a query without parameter binding.
Cross-site scripting executes attacker JavaScript in a victim's browser through unescaped output.
Mutual TLS authenticates both client and server, blocking unauthorized callers at the transport layer.
Role-based access control assigns permissions to roles and roles to users.
Audit logs record who did what and when so a breach can be investigated after the fact.
A process is an isolated execution context with its own virtual address space.
Threads within a process share memory and file descriptors but have separate stacks.
The scheduler decides which runnable thread to put on each CPU core next.
Virtual memory uses page tables to map process addresses to physical RAM or swap.
A system call traps from user mode into the kernel to request privileged services.
Mutexes serialize access to shared data so only one thread enters the critical section at a time.
File descriptors are kernel-side integers that user processes use to identify open files and sockets.
Copy-on-write makes fork cheap by sharing pages until one of the processes writes to them.
Signals deliver asynchronous notifications to a process, like SIGTERM for graceful shutdown.
Cgroups constrain CPU, memory, and I/O for groups of processes on Linux.
